PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-05-2017 Continued To Aug-16-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ciarin O'Sullivan for Noreen Hoi (Petitioner).

Mr. O'Sullivan stated that the time to debate whether any of property is the property of Ernesto is when his Client gets appointed, that there were two Trusts (1994 and 2015), that the Husband and Wife both died within two weeks, that many accounts are properly named in the 1994 Trust, and that there a number of assets in the name of Vangum which they can transfer pursuant to the pour over will. Mr. O'Sullivan also stated that most of the known heirs are Vangum's, that they all received notice, that two of Ernesto's siblings have died, that there is the issue of whether they should give notice to the three Sisters in China, that they don't know if they are alive, that they don't know where they are in China and that they don't know the proper spelling of their names. The Court noted that box 7.b.(1) (decedent had a predeceased spouse who died not more than 15 years before decedent and who owned an interest in real property that passed to decedent) on page 3 of the petition was checked. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that there is no question that the property is in the Trust, that he does not recall checking that box and it was done in error, that the house they owned is in the Trust and that there is no real property which passed from decedent to Ernesto. In addition, Mr. O'Sullivan stated that he believes the $200,000.00 in the Estate is the Decedent's separate property, that he can amend the petition to correct the allegation in 7. b, that he is asking for a decision on the request for waiver of notice to Ernesto's siblings, that efforts made in locating them were indicated in the supplement, that his Client does know any Ernesto's decedents, that there was an enormous geographical separation in the family, that his Client spoke with all of the Vangum heirs and that she does not know where to start. Furthermore, Mr. O'Sullivan stated that he has not inquired as to the cost of hiring an expert to try and locate heirs, that they don't have a copy of Ernesto's will, that it was a very long term marriage, that Vangum once owned property in Canada that was liquidated some time ago, that he is not aware of anything passing/transferring from Ernesto to Vangum, and that the parties have spoken, but that no agreement can be reached as to a neutral third party. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to August 16, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. O'Sullivan is to file a supplement in support the request for waiver of notice.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-06-2017 Continued To Jul-05-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-24-2017 Continued To Jun-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan for Noreen Ho (Petitioner).
Vincent Yu for Pinky Ho (Respondent).

Mr. O'Sullivan stated that he was in communication with the Examiner yesterday, that he had a death in the family and was out of State, and that he filed a supplement to the petition yesterday. Mr. O'Sullivan also stated that he gave notice to the five people listed on the family tree, that there are two separate related Trust matters on calendar for June 6th and asked that this matter be continued to that date. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to June 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
